summary: Found one high-confidence issue in the preview test coverage: the new helper used by preview tests does not preserve the requested accessory capability, so the mixed-capability and full-color assertions do not actually validate the intended planner paths.

findings.title: Preview tests discard requested accessory capabilities
findings.description: `WakeAlarmPlannerPreviewTests.createAccessory` accepts a `capability` argument but constructs `AccessoryReference` with the `isCompatible:` convenience initializer. That initializer always maps compatible accessories to `.brightnessOnly`, so test fixtures created with `.tunableWhite` or `.fullColor` are silently downgraded before `AlarmEditorState.selectedCapabilities` and `WakeAlarmStepPlanner.planSteps(for:capabilities:)` run.
findings.impact: The added preview tests for mixed-capability degradation and same-capability full-color behavior are not exercising the capability-aware preview logic they claim to cover. This can let regressions in degradation messaging or color-mode planning slip through while the suite still passes.
findings.recommendation: Build test fixtures with the `AccessoryReference(..., capability:)` initializer or another path that preserves the requested capability, then keep the mixed-capability/full-color assertions against the real derived capabilities.

sharedStateObservations: `AccessoryReference` has two initializers: one that accepts an explicit `capability`, and a testing convenience initializer with `isCompatible` that always stores `.brightnessOnly` for compatible fixtures.
